heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Richard Linklater. Written by Richard Linklater, Ethan Hawke, Julie Delpy, and Kim Krizan.

heading: Jesse
raw: I'm designed to feel slightly dissatisfied.
clean: I'm designed to feel slightly dissatisfied.

heading: Jesse
raw: Maybe it's just a sense of entitlement. You know, like whenever you feel like you deserve that new pair of shoes. It's OK to want things as long as you don't get pissed off if you don't get 'em. Right?
clean: Maybe it's just a sense of entitlement. You know, like whenever you feel like you deserve that new pair of shoes. It's OK to want things as long as you don't get pissed off if you don't get 'em. Right?

heading: Jesse
raw: Life's hard. It's supposed to be. If we didn't suffer, we wouldn't learn a thing.
clean: Life's hard. It's supposed to be. If we didn't suffer, we wouldn't learn a thing.

heading: Jesse
raw: You realize that most of the people that you meet are trying to get somewhere better, they're trying to make a little bit more cash, trying to get a little more respect, have more people admire them. It's just exhausting.

heading: Jesse
raw: I have this idea of my best self, and I wanted to pursue that even if it might have been overriding my honest self.
clean: I have this idea of my best self, and I wanted to pursue that even if it might have been overriding my honest self.

heading: Jesse
raw: In the moment I remember thinking that it didn't much matter the "Who" of it all. Nobody is gonna be everything to you, and that ultimately it's just a simple action of committing yourself, meeting your responsibilities that matters.

heading: Jesse

heading: Jesse

heading: Jesse

heading: Jesse
raw: There's gotta be something more to love than commitment.

heading: Celine
raw: Memories are wonderful things, if you don't have to deal with the past.

heading: Celine
raw: They enjoy the goal but not the process. But the reality of it is that the true work of improving things is in the little achievements of the day.

heading: Celine

heading: Celine

clean: The concept is absurd. The idea that we can only be complete with another person is evil! Right?

heading: Celine
